title: Fractions, Decimals, Ratios, and Proportional Structure · Worked Case: Decimal Conversion
summary: Scale recipes, medication-free training examples, mixtures, maps, and staffing plans without changing relationships. Planned artifact: Add and verify the math beg decimal conversion increment in a proportional scaling and comparison workbook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Convert among fractions, decimals, and terminating or repeating representations without losing meaning.
Add, subtract, multiply, and divide fractions by reasoning about units and equivalent forms.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

steps.title: Calculate Decimal Conversion evidence
steps.instruction: A measured quantity starts at 21 units, changes by −8 units, then by +6 units. Calculate the final quantity.
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Preserve the signs and compute 21 − 8 + 6; compare with the starting scale.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.

hints: Preserve the signs and compute 21 − 8 + 6; compare with the starting scale.
